examples.title: File Operations in C
examples.description: Demonstrates basic file operations: create, open, write, read, seek, close
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates POSIX file operations using system calls: open() to create/open files, write() to write data, read() to read data, lseek() to move file pointer, and close() to release file descriptor.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for read/write where n is bytes transferred

examples.title: File Operations in Python
examples.description: High-level file operations demonstrating sequential and random access
examples.code:
# OPEN FILE FOR WRITING
with open('data.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write('Line 1: Hello\n')
    f.write('Line 2: World\n')
    f.write('Line 3: File Concepts\n')

# OPEN FILE FOR READING (Sequential Access)
with open('data.txt', 'r') as f:
    for line in f:  # Sequential access
        print(line.strip())

# RANDOM ACCESS USING SEEK
with open('data.txt', 'r') as f:
    f.seek(12)  # Jump to byte 12
    print(f.read(5))  # Read 5 bytes from position
    
    f.seek(0, 0)  # Seek to beginning (SEEK_SET)
    first_line = f.readline()
    
    f.seek(0, 2)  # Seek to end (SEEK_END)
    position = f.tell()

print(f"File size: {position} bytes")
examples.explanation: Python provides high-level file operations. The with statement ensures proper file closing. seek() enables random access by moving to specific byte positions. tell() returns current position.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for buffer used during operations

examples.title: File Operations in Java
examples.description: Java file I/O with buffered streams for efficient processing
examples.explanation: Java uses BufferedReader/BufferedWriter for efficient I/O with buffering. RandomAccessFile class provides random access capability with seek() method. try-with-resources ensures proper resource cleanup.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for read/write where n is characters/bytes
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) buffer size for Buffered classes
